verses.latin_vulgate: In anno secundo regni Nabuchodonosor, vidit Nabuchodonosor somnium, et conterritus est spiritus ejus, et somnium ejus fugit ab eo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבִשְׁנַ֣ת שְׁתַּ֗יִם לְמַלְכוּת֙ נְבֻֽכַדְנֶצַּ֔ר חָלַ֥ם נְבֻֽכַדְנֶצַּ֖ר חֲלֹמ֑וֹת וַתִּתְפָּ֣עֶם רוּח֔וֹ וּשְׁנָת֖וֹ נִהְיְתָ֥ה עָלָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: In the second year of the reign of Nabuchodonosor, Nabuchodonosor had a dream, and his spirit was terrified, and his dream went out of his mind.
verses.finnish_biblia: Toisena Nebukadnetsarin valtakunnan vuotena näki Nebukadnetsar unta, josta hän hämmästyi, niin että hän heräsi.
verses.king_james: And in the second year of the reign of Nebuchadnezzar Nebuchadnezzar dreamed dreams, wherewith his spirit was troubled, and his sleep brake from him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Præcepit autem rex ut convocarentur arioli, et magi, et malefici, et Chaldæi, ut indicarent regi somnia sua. Qui cum venissent, steterunt coram rege.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר הַ֠מֶּ֠לֶךְ לִקְרֹ֨א לַֽחַרְטֻמִּ֜ים וְלָֽאַשָּׁפִ֗ים וְלַֽמְכַשְּׁפִים֙ וְלַכַּשְׂדִּ֔ים לְהַגִּ֥יד לַמֶּ֖לֶךְ חֲלֹמֹתָ֑יו וַיָּבֹ֕אוּ וַיַּֽעַמְד֖וּ לִפְנֵ֥י הַמֶּֽלֶךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then the king commanded to call together the diviners and the wise men, and the magicians, and the Chaldeans: to declare to the king his dreams: so they came and stood before the king.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuningas käski tähtientutkiat ja viisaat ja noidat ja Kaldealaiset kutsuttaa kokoon, sanomaan kuninkaalle hänen untansa. Ja he tulivat ja astuivat kuninkaan eteen.
verses.king_james: Then the king commanded to call the magicians, and the astrologers, and the sorcerers, and the Chaldeans, for to shew the king his dreams. So they came and stood before the king.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et dixit ad eos rex : Vidi somnium, et mente confusus ignoro quid viderim.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֧אמֶר לָהֶ֛ם הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ חֲל֣וֹם חָלָ֑מְתִּי וַתִּפָּ֣עֶם רוּחִ֔י לָדַ֖עַת אֶֽת־הַחֲלֽוֹם׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king said to them: I saw a dream: and being troubled in mind I know not what I saw.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuningas sanoi heille: minä näin unta, josta minä peljästyin, ja minä mielelläni tahtoisin tietää, mikä se uni oli.
verses.king_james: And the king said unto them, I have dreamed a dream, and my spirit was troubled to know the dream.

verses.latin_vulgate: Responderuntque Chaldæi regi syriace : Rex, in sempiternum vive ! dic somnium servis tuis, et interpretationem ejus indicabimus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַֽיְדַבְּר֧וּ הַכַּשְׂדִּ֛ים לַמֶּ֖לֶךְ אֲרָמִ֑ית מַלְכָּא֙ לְעָלְמִ֣ין חֱיִ֔י אֱמַ֥ר חֶלְמָ֛א (לעבדיך) [לְעַבְדָ֖ךְ] וּפִשְׁרָ֥א נְחַוֵּֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the Chaldeans answered the king in Syriac: O king, live for ever: tell to thy servants thy dream, and we will declare the interpretation thereof.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoivat Kaldealaiset kuninkaalle Syrian kielellä: kuningas eläköön kauvan! sano palvelioilles uni, niin me tahdomme selittää sen.
verses.king_james: Then spake the Chaldeans to the king in Syriack, O king, live for ever: tell thy servants the dream, and we will shew the interpretation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondens rex ait Chaldæis : Sermo recessit a me : nisi indicaveritis mihi somnium, et conjecturam ejus, peribitis vos, et domus vestræ publicabuntur.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָנֵ֤ה מַלְכָּא֙ וְאָמַ֣ר (לכשדיא) [לְכַשְׂדָּאֵ֔י] מִלְּתָ֖ה מִנִּ֣י אַזְדָּ֑א הֵ֣ן לָ֤א תְהֽוֹדְעוּנַּ֙נִי֙ חֶלְמָ֣א וּפִשְׁרֵ֔הּ הַדָּמִין֙ תִּתְעַבְד֔וּן וּבָתֵּיכ֖וֹן נְוָלִ֥י יִתְּשָׂמֽוּן׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king, answering, said to the Chaldeans: The thing is gone out of my mind: unless you tell me the dream, and the meaning thereof, you shall be put to death, and your houses shall be confiscated.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuningas vastasi ja sanoi Kaldealaisille: minä olen unhottanut; jollette minulle ilmoita unta ja selitä sitä, niin te pitää kappaleiksi hakattaman, ja teidän huoneenne lokaläjäksi kukistettaman.
verses.king_james: The king answered and said to the Chaldeans, The thing is gone from me: if ye will not make known unto me the dream, with the interpretation thereof, ye shall be cut in pieces, and your houses shall be made a dunghill.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si autem somnium, et conjecturam ejus narraveritis, præmia, et dona, et honorem multum accipietis a me. Somnium igitur, et interpretationem ejus indicate mihi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהֵ֨ן חֶלְמָ֤א וּפִשְׁרֵהּ֙ תְּֽהַחֲוֺ֔ן מַתְּנָ֤ן וּנְבִזְבָּה֙ וִיקָ֣ר שַׂגִּ֔יא תְּקַבְּל֖וּן מִן־קֳדָמָ֑י לָהֵ֕ן חֶלְמָ֥א וּפִשְׁרֵ֖הּ הַחֲוֺֽנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: but if you tell the dream, and the meaning of it, you shall receive of me rewards, and gifts, and great honour: therefore, tell me the dream, and the interpretation thereof.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaan jos te minun tietää annatte unen ja selityksen, niin teidän pitää saaman lahjoja, antimia ja suuren kunnian minulta: sentähden sanokaat minulle uni ja sen selitys.
verses.king_james: But if ye shew the dream, and the interpretation thereof, ye shall receive of me gifts and rewards and great honour: therefore shew me the dream, and the interpretation thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Responderunt secundo, atque dixerunt : Rex somnium dicat servis suis, et interpretationem illius indicabimus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֲנ֥וֹ תִנְיָנ֖וּת וְאָמְרִ֑ין מַלְכָּ֕א חֶלְמָ֛א יֵאמַ֥ר לְעַבְד֖וֹהִי וּפִשְׁרָ֥ה נְהַחֲוֵֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: They answered again and said: Let the king tell his servants the dream, and we will declare the interpretation of it.
verses.finnish_biblia: He vastasivat jälleen ja sanoivat: kuningas sanokaan palvelioillensa unen, me tahdomme sen selittää.
verses.king_james: They answered again and said, Let the king tell his servants the dream, and we will shew the interpretation of it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit rex, et ait : Certe novi quod tempus redimitis, scientes quod recesserit a me sermo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָנֵ֤ה מַלְכָּא֙ וְאָמַ֔ר מִן־יַצִּיב֙ יָדַ֣ע אֲנָ֔ה דִּ֥י עִדָּנָ֖א אַנְתּ֣וּן זָבְנִ֑ין כׇּל־קֳבֵל֙ דִּ֣י חֲזֵית֔וֹן דִּ֥י אַזְדָּ֖א מִנִּ֥י מִלְּתָֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: The king answered and said: I know for certain, that you seek to gain time, since you know that the thing is gone from me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuningas vastasi ja sanoi: totisesti minä ymmärrän, että te aikaa kulutatte, koska te näette minun sen unohtaneeksi.
verses.king_james: The king answered and said, I know of certainty that ye would gain the time, because ye see the thing is gone from me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si ergo somnium non indicaveritis mihi, una est de vobis sententia, quod interpretationem quoque fallacem, et deceptione plenam composueritis, ut loquamini mihi donec tempus pertranseat. Somnium itaque dicite mihi, ut sciam quod interpretationem quoque ejus veram loquamini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דִּ֣י הֵן־חֶלְמָא֩ לָ֨א תְהֽוֹדְעֻנַּ֜נִי חֲדָה־הִ֣יא דָֽתְכ֗וֹן וּמִלָּ֨ה כִדְבָ֤ה וּשְׁחִיתָה֙ (הזמנתון) [הִזְדְּמִנְתּוּן֙] לְמֵאמַ֣ר קׇֽדָמַ֔י עַ֛ד דִּ֥י עִדָּנָ֖א יִשְׁתַּנֵּ֑א לָהֵ֗ן חֶלְמָא֙ אֱמַ֣רוּ לִ֔י וְֽאִנְדַּ֕ע דִּ֥י פִשְׁרֵ֖הּ תְּהַחֲוֻנַּֽנִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: If, therefore, you tell me not the dream, there is one sentence concerning you, that you have also framed a lying interpretation, and full of deceit, to speak before me till the time pass away. Tell me, therefore, the dream, that I may know that you also give a true interpretation thereof.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta jollette minulle unta sano, niin tuomio käy teidän ylitsenne, että te olette aikoneet minun edessäni puhua vääryyttä, siihenasti että aika kuluis: sentähden sanokaat minulle uni, niin minä ymmärrän, että te myös selityksen osaatte.
verses.king_james: But if ye will not make known unto me the dream, there is but one decree for you: for ye have prepared lying and corrupt words to speak before me, till the time be changed: therefore tell me the dream, and I shall know that ye can shew me the interpretation thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondentes ergo Chaldæi coram rege, dixerunt : Non est homo super terram, qui sermonem tuum, rex, possit implere : sed neque regum quisquam magnus et potens verbum hujuscemodi sciscitatur ab omni ariolo, et mago, et Chaldæo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עֲנ֨וֹ (כשדיא) [כַשְׂדָּאֵ֤י] קֳדָם־מַלְכָּא֙ וְאָ֣מְרִ֔ין לָֽא־אִיתַ֤י אֱנָשׁ֙ עַל־יַבֶּשְׁתָּ֔א דִּ֚י מִלַּ֣ת מַלְכָּ֔א יוּכַ֖ל לְהַחֲוָיָ֑ה כׇּל־קֳבֵ֗ל דִּ֚י כׇּל־מֶ֙לֶךְ֙ רַ֣ב וְשַׁלִּ֔יט מִלָּ֤ה כִדְנָה֙ לָ֣א שְׁאֵ֔ל לְכׇל־חַרְטֹ֖ם וְאָשַׁ֥ף וְכַשְׂדָּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then the Chaldeans answered before the king, and said: There is no man upon earth, that can accomplish thy word, O king; neither doth any king, though great and mighty, ask such a thing of any diviner, or wise man, or Chaldean.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin vastasivat Kaldealaiset kuninkaan edessä ja sanoivat: ei yhtään ihmistä ole maan päällä, joka sanoa taitaa, mitä kuningas anoo; niin ei ole myös yhtään kuningasta, kuinka suuri ja voimallinen hän olis, joka senkaltaista tähtientutkialta, viisaalta eli Kaldealaiselta vaatii.
verses.king_james: The Chaldeans answered before the king, and said, There is not a man upon the earth that can shew the king’s matter: therefore there is no king, lord, nor ruler, that asked such things at any magician, or astrologer, or Chaldean.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sermo enim, quem tu quæris, rex, gravis est : nec reperietur quisquam qui indicet illum in conspectu regis, exceptis diis, quorum non est cum hominibus conversatio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמִלְּתָ֨א דִֽי־מַלְכָּ֤ה שָׁאֵל֙ יַקִּירָ֔ה וְאׇחֳרָן֙ לָ֣א אִיתַ֔י דִּ֥י יְחַוִּנַּ֖הּ קֳדָ֣ם מַלְכָּ֑א לָהֵ֣ן אֱלָהִ֔ין דִּ֚י מְדָ֣רְה֔וֹן עִם־בִּשְׂרָ֖א לָ֥א אִיתֽוֹהִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: For the thing that thou asketh, O king, is difficult: nor can any one be found that can shew it before the king, except the gods, whose conversation is not with men.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä se, jota kuningas anoo, on ylen korkia, ja ei yksikään ole, joka sen kuninkaalle sanoa taitaa, paitsi jumalia, jotka ihmisten seassa ei asu.
verses.king_james: And it is a rare thing that the king requireth, and there is none other that can shew it before the king, except the gods, whose dwelling is not with flesh.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quo audito, rex, in furore et in ira magna, præcepit ut perirent omnes sapientes Babylonis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּל־קֳבֵ֣ל דְּנָ֔ה מַלְכָּ֕א בְּנַ֖ס וּקְצַ֣ף שַׂגִּ֑יא וַאֲמַר֙ לְה֣וֹבָדָ֔ה לְכֹ֖ל חַכִּימֵ֥י בָבֶֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Upon hearing this, the king in fury, and in great wrath, commanded that all the wise men of Babylon should be put to death.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin vihastui kuningas sangen suuresti, ja käski kaikki viisaat Babelissa tapettaa.
verses.king_james: For this cause the king was angry and very furious, and commanded to destroy all the wise men of Babylon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et egressa sententia, sapientes interficiebantur : quærebanturque Daniel et socii ejus, ut perirent.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְדָתָ֣א נֶפְקַ֔ת וְחַכִּֽימַיָּ֖א מִֽתְקַטְּלִ֑ין וּבְע֛וֹ דָּנִיֵּ֥אל וְחַבְר֖וֹהִי לְהִתְקְטָלָֽה׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: And the decree being gone forth, the wise men were slain: and Daniel and his companions were sought for, to be put to death.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja tuomio annettiin, että viisaat tapettaman piti; ja Danielia hänen kumppaniensa kanssa myös etsittiin tapettaviksi.
verses.king_james: And the decree went forth that the wise men should be slain; and they sought Daniel and his fellows to be slain.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc Daniel requisivit de lege atque sententia ab Arioch principe militiæ regis, qui egressus fuerat ad interficiendos sapientes Babylonis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בֵּאדַ֣יִן דָּנִיֵּ֗אל הֲתִיב֙ עֵטָ֣א וּטְעֵ֔ם לְאַרְי֕וֹךְ רַב־טַבָּחַיָּ֖א דִּ֣י מַלְכָּ֑א דִּ֚י נְפַ֣ק לְקַטָּלָ֔ה לְחַכִּימֵ֖י בָּבֶֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Daniel inquired concerning the law and the sentence, of Arioch, the general of the king's army, who was gone forth to kill the wise men of Babylon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin saatti Daniel toisen neuvon ja käskyn Ariokille, kuninkaan ylimmäiselle päämiehelle, joka meni ulos tappamaan viisaita Babelissa.
verses.king_james: Then Daniel answered with counsel and wisdom to Arioch the captain of the king’s guard, which was gone forth to slay the wise men of Babylon:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et interrogavit eum, qui a rege potestatem acceperat, quam ob causam tam crudelis sententia a facie regis esset egressa. Cum ergo rem indicasset Arioch Danieli,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָנֵ֣ה וְאָמַ֗ר לְאַרְיוֹךְ֙ שַׁלִּיטָ֣א דִֽי־מַלְכָּ֔א עַל־מָ֥ה דָתָ֛א מְהַחְצְפָ֖ה מִן־קֳדָ֣ם מַלְכָּ֑א אֱדַ֣יִן מִלְּתָ֔א הוֹדַ֥ע אַרְי֖וֹךְ לְדָנִיֵּֽאל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he asked him that had received the orders of the king, why so cruel a sentence was gone forth from the face of the king. And when Arioch had told the matter to Daniel,
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja hän vastasi ja sanoi kuninkaan käskyläiselle Ariokille: miksi niin ankara tuomio on käynyt ulos kuninkaalta? Ja Ariok antoi Danielin tietää sen asian.
verses.king_james: He answered and said to Arioch the king’s captain, Why is the decree so hasty from the king? Then Arioch made the thing known to Daniel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Daniel ingressus rogavit regem ut tempus daret sibi ad solutionem indicandam regi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְדָ֣נִיֵּ֔אל עַ֖ל וּבְעָ֣ה מִן־מַלְכָּ֑א דִּ֚י זְמָ֣ן יִנְתִּן־לֵ֔הּ וּפִשְׁרָ֖א לְהַֽחֲוָיָ֥ה לְמַלְכָּֽא׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: Daniel went in, and desired of the king, that he would give him time to resolve the question, and declare it to the king.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin meni Daniel ylös ja rukoili kuningasta, että hän antais hänelle aikaa, sanoaksensa kuninkaalle selitystä.
verses.king_james: Then Daniel went in, and desired of the king that he would give him time, and that he would shew the king the interpretation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ingressus est domum suam, Ananiæque et Misaëli et Azariæ, sociis suis, indicavit negotium,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱדַ֥יִן דָּנִיֵּ֖אל לְבַיְתֵ֣הּ אֲזַ֑ל וְ֠לַחֲנַנְיָ֠ה מִֽישָׁאֵ֧ל וַעֲזַרְיָ֛ה חַבְר֖וֹהִי מִלְּתָ֥א הוֹדַֽע׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he went into his house, and told the matter to Ananias, and Misael, and Azarias, his companions:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Daniel meni kotia ja ilmoitti sen kumppaneillensa Hananialle, Misaelille ja Asarialle,
verses.king_james: Then Daniel went to his house, and made the thing known to Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah, his companions:

verses.latin_vulgate: ut quærerent misericordiam a facie Dei cæli super sacramento isto, et non perirent Daniel et socii ejus cum ceteris sapientibus Babylonis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְרַחֲמִ֗ין לְמִבְעֵא֙ מִן־קֳדָם֙ אֱלָ֣הּ שְׁמַיָּ֔א עַל־רָזָ֖א דְּנָ֑ה דִּ֣י לָ֤א יְהֽוֹבְדוּן֙ דָּנִיֵּ֣אל וְחַבְר֔וֹהִי עִם־שְׁאָ֖ר חַכִּימֵ֥י בָבֶֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: To the end that they should ask mercy at the face of the God of heaven, concerning this secret, and that Daniel and his companions might not perish with the rest of the wise men of Babylon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Että he taivaan Jumalalta armoa rukoilisivat sen salaisen asian tähden, ettei Daniel kumppaninensa ynnä muiden viisasten kanssa Babelissa hukkuisi.
verses.king_james: That they would desire mercies of the God of heaven concerning this secret; that Daniel and his fellows should not perish with the rest of the wise men of Babylon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc Danieli mysterium per visionem nocte revelatum est : et benedixit Daniel Deum cæli,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱדַ֗יִן לְדָנִיֵּ֛אל בְּחֶזְוָ֥א דִֽי־לֵילְיָ֖א רָזָ֣א גְלִ֑י אֱדַ֙יִן֙ דָּֽנִיֵּ֔אל בָּרִ֖ךְ לֶאֱלָ֥הּ שְׁמַיָּֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then was the mystery revealed to Daniel by a vision in the night: and Daniel blessed the God of heaven,
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin ilmoitettiin Danielille se salainen asia yöllä näyssä: niin Daniel kiitti Jumalaa taivaasta,
verses.king_james: Then was the secret revealed unto Daniel in a night vision. Then Daniel blessed the God of heaven.

verses.latin_vulgate: et locutus ait : Sit nomen Domini benedictum a sæculo et usque in sæculum : quia sapientia et fortitudo ejus sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָנֵ֤ה דָֽנִיֵּאל֙ וְאָמַ֔ר לֶהֱוֵ֨א שְׁמֵ֤הּ דִּֽי־אֱלָהָא֙ מְבָרַ֔ךְ מִן־עָלְמָ֖א וְעַ֣ד עָלְמָ֑א דִּ֧י חׇכְמְתָ֛א וּגְבוּרְתָ֖א דִּ֥י לֵֽהּ־הִֽיא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And speaking, he said: Blessed be the name of the Lord from eternity and for evermore: for wisdom and fortitude are his.
verses.finnish_biblia: Vastasi ja sanoi: kiitetty olkoon Jumalan nimi ijankaikkisesta ijankaikkiseen! sillä hänen on viisaus ja väkevyys.
verses.king_james: Daniel answered and said, Blessed be the name of God for ever and ever: for wisdom and might are his:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ipse mutat tempora, et ætates : transfert regna, atque constituit : dat sapientiam sapientibus, et scientiam intelligentibus disciplinam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְ֠ה֠וּא מְהַשְׁנֵ֤א עִדָּנַיָּא֙ וְזִמְנַיָּ֔א מְהַעְדֵּ֥ה מַלְכִ֖ין וּמְהָקֵ֣ים מַלְכִ֑ין יָהֵ֤ב חׇכְמְתָא֙ לְחַכִּימִ֔ין וּמַנְדְּעָ֖א לְיָדְעֵ֥י בִינָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he changeth times and ages: taketh away kingdoms, and establisheth them: giveth wisdom to the wise, and knowledge to them that have understanding:
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän muuttaa ajat ja hetket; panee pois kuninkaat ja asettaa kuninkaat; hän antaa viisaille viisauden ja ymmärtäväisille taidon ja ymmärryksen.
verses.king_james: And he changeth the times and the seasons: he removeth kings, and setteth up kings: he giveth wisdom unto the wise, and knowledge to them that know understanding:

verses.latin_vulgate: Ipse revelat profunda et abscondita, et novit in tenebris constituta : et lux cum eo est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֛וּא גָּלֵ֥א עַמִּיקָתָ֖א וּמְסַתְּרָתָ֑א יָדַע֙ מָ֣ה בַחֲשׁוֹכָ֔א (ונהירא) [וּנְהוֹרָ֖א] עִמֵּ֥הּ שְׁרֵֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: He revealeth deep and hidden things, and knoweth what is in darkness: and light is with him.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän ilmoittaa syvät ja salaiset asiat; hän tietää, mitä pimiässä on, sillä hänen tykönänsä on sula valkeus.
verses.king_james: He revealeth the deep and secret things: he knoweth what is in the darkness, and the light dwelleth with him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tibi, Deus patrum nostrorum, confiteor, teque laudo, quia sapientiam et fortitudinem dedisti mihi, et nunc ostendisti mihi quæ rogavimus te, quia sermonem regis aperuisti nobis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָ֣ךְ ׀ אֱלָ֣הּ אֲבָהָתִ֗י מְהוֹדֵ֤א וּמְשַׁבַּח֙ אֲנָ֔ה דִּ֧י חׇכְמְתָ֛א וּגְבוּרְתָ֖א יְהַ֣בְתְּ לִ֑י וּכְעַ֤ן הֽוֹדַעְתַּ֙נִי֙ דִּֽי־בְעֵ֣ינָא מִנָּ֔ךְ דִּֽי־מִלַּ֥ת מַלְכָּ֖א הוֹדַעְתֶּֽנָא׃
verses.douay_rheims: To thee, O God of our fathers, I give thanks, and I praise thee: because thou hast given me wisdom and strength: and now thou hast shewn me what we desired of thee, for thou hast made known to us the king's discourse.
verses.finnish_biblia: Minä kiitän ja ylistän sinua minun isäini Jumala, ettäs minulle viisautta ja väkevyyttä lainaat, ja nyt minulle ilmoittanut olet, jota me sinulta rukoilimme; sinä olet meille kuninkaan asian ilmoittanut.
verses.king_james: I thank thee, and praise thee, O thou God of my fathers, who hast given me wisdom and might, and hast made known unto me now what we desired of thee: for thou hast now made known unto us the king’s matter.

verses.latin_vulgate: Post hæc Daniel ingressus ad Arioch, quem constituerat rex ut perderet sapientes Babylonis, sic ei locutus est : Sapientes Babylonis ne perdas : introduc me in conspectu regis, et solutionem regi narrabo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּל־קֳבֵ֣ל דְּנָ֗ה דָּֽנִיֵּאל֙ עַ֣ל עַל־אַרְי֔וֹךְ דִּ֚י מַנִּ֣י מַלְכָּ֔א לְהוֹבָדָ֖ה לְחַכִּימֵ֣י בָבֶ֑ל אֲזַ֣ל ׀ וְכֵ֣ן אֲמַר־לֵ֗הּ לְחַכִּימֵ֤י בָבֶל֙ אַל־תְּהוֹבֵ֔ד הַעֵ֙לְנִי֙ קֳדָ֣ם מַלְכָּ֔א וּפִשְׁרָ֖א לְמַלְכָּ֥א אֲחַוֵּֽא׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: After this Daniel went in to Arioch, to whom the king had given orders to destroy the wise men of Babylon, and he spoke thus to him: Destroy not the wise men of Babylon: bring me in before the king, and I will tell the solution to the king.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin meni Daniel Ariokin tykö, jolla kuninkaalta käsky oli viisaita Babelissa hukuttaa; hän meni ja sanoi hänelle näin: älä tapa viisaita Babelissa, vaan vie minut ylös kuninkaan tykö, minä tahdon kuninkaalle selityksen sanoa.
verses.king_james: Therefore Daniel went in unto Arioch, whom the king had ordained to destroy the wise men of Babylon: he went and said thus unto him; Destroy not the wise men of Babylon: bring me in before the king, and I will shew unto the king the interpretation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc Arioch festinus introduxit Danielem ad regem, et dixit ei : Inveni hominem de filiis transmigrationis Juda, qui solutionem regi annuntiet.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱדַ֤יִן אַרְיוֹךְ֙ בְּהִתְבְּהָלָ֔ה הַנְעֵ֥ל לְדָנִיֵּ֖אל קֳדָ֣ם מַלְכָּ֑א וְכֵ֣ן אֲמַר־לֵ֗הּ דִּֽי־הַשְׁכַּ֤חַת גְּבַר֙ מִן־בְּנֵ֤י גָֽלוּתָא֙ דִּ֣י יְה֔וּד דִּ֥י פִשְׁרָ֖א לְמַלְכָּ֥א יְהוֹדַֽע׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then Arioch in haste brought in Daniel to the king, and said to him: I have found a man of the children of the captivity of Juda, that will resolve the question to the king.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ariok vei Danielin kiiiruusti kuninkaan eteen, ja sanoi hänelle näin: minä olen löytänyt yhden miehen Juudalaisten vankien seasta, joka kuninkaalle selityksen sanoa taitaa.
verses.king_james: Then Arioch brought in Daniel before the king in haste, and said thus unto him, I have found a man of the captives of Judah, that will make known unto the king the interpretation.

verses.latin_vulgate: Respondit rex, et dixit Danieli, cujus nomen erat Baltassar : Putasne vere potes mihi indicare somnium, quod vidi, et interpretationem ejus ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָנֵ֤ה מַלְכָּא֙ וְאָמַ֣ר לְדָנִיֵּ֔אל דִּ֥י שְׁמֵ֖הּ בֵּלְטְשַׁאצַּ֑ר (האיתיך) [הַֽאִיתָ֣ךְ] כָּהֵ֗ל לְהוֹדָעֻתַ֛נִי חֶלְמָ֥א דִֽי־חֲזֵ֖ית וּפִשְׁרֵֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The king answered, and said to Daniel, whose name was Baltassar: Thinkest thou indeed that thou canst tell me the dream that I saw, and the interpretation thereof?
verses.finnish_biblia: Kuningas vastasi ja sanoi Danielille, joka Belsatsariksi kutsuttiin: oletko sinä se, joka minulle sen unen, jonka minä nähnyt olen, ja hänen selityksensä ilmoittaa taidat?
verses.king_james: The king answered and said to Daniel, whose name was Belteshazzar, Art thou able to make known unto me the dream which I have seen, and the interpretation thereof?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondens Daniel coram rege, ait : Mysterium, quod rex interrogat, sapientes, magi, arioli, et aruspices nequeunt indicare regi :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָנֵ֧ה דָנִיֵּ֛אל קֳדָ֥ם מַלְכָּ֖א וְאָמַ֑ר רָזָא֙ דִּֽי־מַלְכָּ֣א שָׁאֵ֔ל לָ֧א חַכִּימִ֣ין אָֽשְׁפִ֗ין חַרְטֻמִּין֙ גָּזְרִ֔ין יָכְלִ֖ין לְהַֽחֲוָיָ֥ה לְמַלְכָּֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Daniel made answer before the king, and said: The secret that the king desireth to know, none of the wise men, or the philosophers, or the diviners, or the soothsayers, can declare to the king.
verses.finnish_biblia: Daniel vastasi kuninkaan edessä ja sanoi: sitä salautta, jota kuningas anoo, ei viisaat, tähtientutkiat, oppineet ja noidat taida kuninkaalle ilmoittaa.
verses.king_james: Daniel answered in the presence of the king, and said, The secret which the king hath demanded cannot the wise men, the astrologers, the magicians, the soothsayers, shew unto the king;

verses.latin_vulgate: sed est Deus in cælo revelans mysteria, qui indicavit tibi, rex Nabuchodonosor, quæ ventura sunt in novissimis temporibus. Somnium tuum, et visiones capitis tui in cubili tuo hujuscemodi sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בְּרַ֡ם אִיתַ֞י אֱלָ֤הּ בִּשְׁמַיָּא֙ גָּלֵ֣א רָזִ֔ין וְהוֹדַ֗ע לְמַלְכָּא֙ נְבֽוּכַדְנֶצַּ֔ר מָ֛ה דִּ֥י לֶהֱוֵ֖א בְּאַחֲרִ֣ית יוֹמַיָּ֑א חֶלְמָ֨ךְ וְחֶזְוֵ֥י רֵאשָׁ֛ךְ עַֽל־מִשְׁכְּבָ֖ךְ דְּנָ֥ה הֽוּא׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: But there is a God in heaven that revealeth mysteries, who hath shewn to thee, O king Nabuchodonosor, what is to come to pass in the latter times. Thy dream, and the visions of thy head upon thy bed, are these:
verses.finnish_biblia: Vaan Jumala on taivaissa, joka taitaa salaiset asiat julistaa, hän tiettäväksi teki kuningas Nebukadnetsarille, mitä tulevaisina aikoina tapahtuman pitää. Tämä on sinun unes ja sinun näkys, kuin sinä makasit.
verses.king_james: But there is a God in heaven that revealeth secrets, and maketh known to the king Nebuchadnezzar what shall be in the latter days. Thy dream, and the visions of thy head upon thy bed, are these;

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu, rex, cogitare cœpisti in strato tuo, quid esset futurum post hæc : et qui revelat mysteria, ostendit tibi quæ ventura sunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: (אנתה) [אַ֣נְתְּ] מַלְכָּ֗א רַעְיוֹנָךְ֙ עַל־מִשְׁכְּבָ֣ךְ סְלִ֔קוּ מָ֛ה דִּ֥י לֶהֱוֵ֖א אַחֲרֵ֣י דְנָ֑ה וְגָלֵ֧א רָזַיָּ֛א הוֹדְעָ֖ךְ מָה־דִ֥י לֶהֱוֵֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thou, O king, didst begin to think in thy bed, what should come to pass hereafter: and he that revealeth mysteries shewed thee what shall come to pass.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä kuningas vuoteessas ajattelit mitä tästedes tapahtuman pitäis; ja se, joka salaiset ilmoittaa, hän on sinulle osoittanut, kuinka tapahtuman pitää.
verses.king_james: As for thee, O king, thy thoughts came into thy mind upon thy bed, what should come to pass hereafter: and he that revealeth secrets maketh known to thee what shall come to pass.

verses.latin_vulgate: Mihi quoque non in sapientia, quæ est in me plus quam in cunctis viventibus, sacramentum hoc revelatum est : sed ut interpretatio regi manifesta fieret, et cogitationes mentis tuæ scires.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַאֲנָ֗ה לָ֤א בְחׇכְמָה֙ דִּֽי־אִיתַ֥י בִּי֙ מִן־כׇּל־חַיַּיָּ֔א רָזָ֥א דְנָ֖ה גֱּלִ֣י לִ֑י לָהֵ֗ן עַל־דִּבְרַת֙ דִּ֤י פִשְׁרָא֙ לְמַלְכָּ֣א יְהוֹדְע֔וּן וְרַעְיוֹנֵ֥י לִבְבָ֖ךְ תִּנְדַּֽע׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: To me also this secret is revealed, not by any wisdom that I have more than all men alive: but that the interpretation might be made manifest to the king, and thou mightest know the thought of thy mind.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin ovat senkaltaiset asiat minulle ilmoitetut, ei minun viisauteni tähden, niinkuin se olis suurempi kuin kaikkein, jotka elävät, vaan sentähden että sen selitys pitäis kuninkaalle tiettäväksi tuleman, ja sinä saisit sinun sydämes ajatukset tietää.
verses.king_james: But as for me, this secret is not revealed to me for any wisdom that I have more than any living, but for their sakes that shall make known the interpretation to the king, and that thou mightest know the thoughts of thy heart.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu, rex, videbas, et ecce quasi statua una grandis : statua illa magna, et statura sublimis stabat contra te, et intuitus ejus erat terribilis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: (אנתה) [אַ֣נְתְּ] מַלְכָּ֗א חָזֵ֤ה הֲוַ֙יְתָ֙ וַאֲל֨וּ צְלֵ֥ם חַד֙ שַׂגִּ֔יא צַלְמָ֨א דִכֵּ֥ן רַ֛ב וְזִיוֵ֥הּ יַתִּ֖יר קָאֵ֣ם לְקׇבְלָ֑ךְ וְרֵוֵ֖הּ דְּחִֽיל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thou, O king, sawest, and behold there was as it were a great statue: this statue, which was great and high, tall of stature, stood before thee, and the look thereof was terrible.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä kuningas näit, ja katso, suuri ja korkia kuva seisoi suurella kirkkaudella sinun edessäs; ja se oli hirmuinen nähdä.
verses.king_james: Thou, O king, sawest, and behold a great image. This great image, whose brightness was excellent, stood before thee; and the form thereof was terrible.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hujus statuæ caput ex auro optimo erat, pectus autem et brachia de argento, porro venter et femora ex ære,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ה֣וּא צַלְמָ֗א רֵאשֵׁהּ֙ דִּֽי־דְהַ֣ב טָ֔ב חֲד֥וֹהִי וּדְרָע֖וֹהִי דִּ֣י כְסַ֑ף מְע֥וֹהִי וְיַרְכָתֵ֖הּ דִּ֥י נְחָֽשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: The head of this statue was of fine gold, but the breast and the arms of silver, and the belly and the thighs of brass.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sen kuvan pää oli parhaimmasta kullasta; mutta sen rinta ja käsivarret olivat hopiasta; sen vatsa ja lanteet olivat vaskesta.
verses.king_james: This image’s head was of fine gold, his breast and his arms of silver, his belly and his thighs of brass,

verses.latin_vulgate: tibiæ autem ferreæ : pedum quædam pars erat ferrea, quædam autem fictilis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׁק֖וֹהִי דִּ֣י פַרְזֶ֑ל רַגְל֕וֹהִי (מנהון) [מִנְּהֵין֙] דִּ֣י פַרְזֶ֔ל (ומנהון) [וּמִנְּהֵ֖ין] דִּ֥י חֲסַֽף׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the legs of iron, the feet part of iron and part of clay.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sen sääret olivat raudasta; sen jalat olivat puolittain raudasta ja puolittain savesta.
verses.king_james: His legs of iron, his feet part of iron and part of clay.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videbas ita, donec abscissus est lapis de monte sine manibus : et percussit statuam in pedibus ejus ferreis et fictilibus, et comminuit eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: חָזֵ֣ה הֲוַ֗יְתָ עַ֠ד דִּ֣י הִתְגְּזֶ֤רֶת אֶ֙בֶן֙ דִּי־לָ֣א בִידַ֔יִן וּמְחָ֤ת לְצַלְמָא֙ עַל־רַגְל֔וֹהִי דִּ֥י פַרְזְלָ֖א וְחַסְפָּ֑א וְהַדֵּ֖קֶת הִמּֽוֹן׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thus thou sawest, till a stone was cut out of a mountain without hands: and it struck the statue upon the feet thereof that were of iron and clay, and broke them in pieces.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sen sinä näit, siihenasti kuin yksi kivi temmattiin ilman käsiä, ja löi sen kuvan jalkoihin, jotka raudasta ja savesta olivat, ja murensi ne rikki.
verses.king_james: Thou sawest till that a stone was cut out without hands, which smote the image upon his feet that were of iron and clay, and brake them to pieces.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc contrita sunt pariter ferrum, testa, æs, argentum, et aurum, et redacta quasi in favillam æstivæ areæ, quæ rapta sunt vento, nullusque locus inventus est eis : lapis autem, qui percusserat statuam, factus est mons magnus, et implevit universam terram.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בֵּאדַ֣יִן דָּ֣קוּ כַחֲדָ֡ה פַּרְזְלָא֩ חַסְפָּ֨א נְחָשָׁ֜א כַּסְפָּ֣א וְדַהֲבָ֗א וַהֲווֹ֙ כְּע֣וּר מִן־אִדְּרֵי־קַ֔יִט וּנְשָׂ֤א הִמּוֹן֙ רוּחָ֔א וְכׇל־אֲתַ֖ר לָא־הִשְׁתְּכַ֣ח לְה֑וֹן וְאַבְנָ֣א ׀ דִּֽי־מְחָ֣ת לְצַלְמָ֗א הֲוָ֛ת לְט֥וּר רַ֖ב וּמְלָ֥את כׇּל־אַרְעָֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then was the iron, the clay, the brass, the silver, and the gold broken to pieces together, and became like the chaff of a summer's threshing floor, and they were carried away by the wind: and there was no place found for them: but the stone that struck the statue became a great mountain, and filled the whole earth.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin tulivat ne kaikki muserretuksi, rauta, savi, vaski, hopia ja kulta, ja tulivat niinkuin akanat suviriihessä, ja tuuli vei ne pois, niin ettei niiden siaa ensinkään löydetty; mutta kivi joka kuvaa löi, tuli suureksi vuoreksi, niin että se kokonaan maan täytti.
verses.king_james: Then was the iron, the clay, the brass, the silver, and the gold, broken to pieces together, and became like the chaff of the summer threshingfloors; and the wind carried them away, that no place was found for them: and the stone that smote the image became a great mountain, and filled the whole earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hoc est somnium : interpretationem quoque ejus dicemus coram te, rex.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: דְּנָ֣ה חֶלְמָ֔א וּפִשְׁרֵ֖הּ נֵאמַ֥ר קֳדָם־מַלְכָּֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: This is the dream: we will also tell the interpretation thereof before thee, O king.
verses.finnish_biblia: Tämä on uni; nyt me tahdomme kuninkaalle sen selityksen sanoa:
verses.king_james: This is the dream; and we will tell the interpretation thereof before the king.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tu rex regum es : et Deus cæli regnum, et fortitudinem, et imperium, et gloriam dedit tibi :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: (אנתה) [אַ֣נְתְּ] מַלְכָּ֔א מֶ֖לֶךְ מַלְכַיָּ֑א דִּ֚י אֱלָ֣הּ שְׁמַיָּ֔א מַלְכוּתָ֥א חִסְנָ֛א וְתׇקְפָּ֥א וִֽיקָרָ֖א יְהַב־לָֽךְ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Thou art a king of kings: and the God of heaven hath given thee a kingdom, and strength, and power, and glory:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinä kuningas olet kuningasten kuningas, jolle Jumala taivaasta valtakunnan, voiman, väkevyyden ja kunnian antanut on;
verses.king_james: Thou, O king, art a king of kings: for the God of heaven hath given thee a kingdom, power, and strength, and glory.

verses.latin_vulgate: et omnia, in quibus habitant filii hominum, et bestiæ agri : volucres quoque cæli dedit in manu tua, et sub ditione tua universa constituit : tu es ergo caput aureum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבְכׇל־דִּ֣י (דארין) [דָֽיְרִ֣ין] בְּֽנֵי־אֲ֠נָשָׁ֠א חֵיוַ֨ת בָּרָ֤א וְעוֹף־שְׁמַיָּא֙ יְהַ֣ב בִּידָ֔ךְ וְהַשְׁלְטָ֖ךְ בְּכׇלְּה֑וֹן (אנתה) [אַ֨נְתְּ־]ה֔וּא רֵאשָׁ֖ה דִּ֥י דַהֲבָֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And all places wherein the children of men, and the beasts of the field do dwell: he hath also given the birds of the air into thy hand, and hath put all things under thy power: thou, therefore, art the head of gold.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kaikki, joissa ihmisen lapset asuvat, ja eläimet kedolla ja linnut taivaan alla on hän sinun käsiis antanut, ja sinulle näiden kaikkein päälle lainasi vallan: sinä se kultainen pää olet.
verses.king_james: And wheresoever the children of men dwell, the beasts of the field and the fowls of the heaven hath he given into thine hand, and hath made thee ruler over them all. Thou art this head of gold.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et post te consurget regnum aliud minus te argenteum : et regnum tertium aliud æreum, quod imperabit universæ terræ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּבָתְרָ֗ךְ תְּק֛וּם מַלְכ֥וּ אׇחֳרִ֖י (ארעא) [אֲרַ֣ע] מִנָּ֑ךְ וּמַלְכ֨וּ (תליתיא) [תְלִיתָאָ֤ה] אׇחֳרִי֙ דִּ֣י נְחָשָׁ֔א דִּ֥י תִשְׁלַ֖ט בְּכׇל־אַרְעָֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And after thee shall rise up another kingdom, inferior to thee, of silver: and another third kingdom of brass, which shall rule over all the world.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sinun jälkees pitää toisen valtakunnan tuleman, halvemman kuin sinä; sitte se kolmas valtakunta, joka vaskinen on, jonka pitää kaikki maakunnat hallitseman.
verses.king_james: And after thee shall arise another kingdom inferior to thee, and another third kingdom of brass, which shall bear rule over all the earth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et regnum quartum erit velut ferrum : quomodo ferrum comminuit, et domat omnia, sic comminuet, et conteret omnia hæc.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמַלְכוּ֙ (רביעיה) [רְבִ֣יעָאָ֔ה] תֶּהֱוֵ֥א תַקִּיפָ֖ה כְּפַרְזְלָ֑א כׇּל־קֳבֵ֗ל דִּ֤י פַרְזְלָא֙ מְהַדֵּ֤ק וְחָשֵׁל֙ כֹּ֔לָּא וּֽכְפַרְזְלָ֛א דִּֽי־מְרָעַ֥ע כׇּל־אִלֵּ֖ן תַּדִּ֥ק וְתֵרֹֽעַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the fourth kingdom shall be as iron. As iron breaketh into pieces, and subdueth all things, so shall that break, and destroy all these.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sen neljännen valtakunnan pitää kovan oleman niinkuin raudan; sillä niinkuin rauta särkee ja murentaa kaikki, ja niinkuin rauta kaikki rikkoo, juuri niin tämän piti myös särkemän ja murentaman.
verses.king_james: And the fourth kingdom shall be strong as iron: forasmuch as iron breaketh in pieces and subdueth all things: and as iron that breaketh all these, shall it break in pieces and bruise.

verses.latin_vulgate: Porro quia vidisti pedum, et digitorum partem testæ figuli, et partem ferream, regnum divisum erit : quod tamen de plantario ferri orietur, secundum quod vidisti ferrum mistum testæ ex luto.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְדִֽי־חֲזַ֜יְתָה רַגְלַיָּ֣א וְאֶצְבְּעָתָ֗א (מנהון) [מִנְּהֵ֞ן] חֲסַ֤ף דִּֽי־פֶחָר֙ (ומנהון) [וּמִנְּהֵ֣ין] פַּרְזֶ֔ל מַלְכ֤וּ פְלִיגָה֙ תֶּהֱוֵ֔ה וּמִן־נִצְבְּתָ֥א דִֽי־פַרְזְלָ֖א לֶֽהֱוֵא־בַ֑הּ כׇּל־קֳבֵל֙ דִּ֣י חֲזַ֔יְתָה פַּ֨רְזְלָ֔א מְעָרַ֖ב בַּחֲסַ֥ף טִינָֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And whereas thou sawest the feet, and the toes, part of potter's clay, and part of iron: the kingdom shall be divided, but yet it shall take its origin from the iron, according as thou sawest the iron mixed with the miry clay.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta ettäs näit jalat ja varpaat, puolittain savesta ja puolittain raudasta, sen pitää jaetun valtakunnan oleman; kuitenkin pitää raudan vahvuudesta siihen jäämän, niinkuin sinä näit raudan olevan savella sekoitetun.
verses.king_james: And whereas thou sawest the feet and toes, part of potters’ clay, and part of iron, the kingdom shall be divided; but there shall be in it of the strength of the iron, forasmuch as thou sawest the iron mixed with miry clay.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et digitos pedum ex parte ferreos, et ex parte fictiles : ex parte regnum erit solidum, et ex parte contritum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאֶצְבְּעָת֙ רַגְלַיָּ֔א (מנהון) [מִנְּהֵ֥ין] פַּרְזֶ֖ל (ומנהון) [וּמִנְּהֵ֣ין] חֲסַ֑ף מִן־קְצָ֤ת מַלְכוּתָא֙ תֶּהֱוֵ֣ה תַקִּיפָ֔ה וּמִנַּ֖הּ תֶּהֱוֵ֥א תְבִירָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And as the toes of the feet were part of iron, and part of clay: the kingdom shall be partly strong, and partly broken.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja että varpaat sen jaloissa puolittain rautaa ja puolittain savea oli, niin sen pitää puolittain vahvan ja puolittain heikon valtakunnan oleman.
verses.king_james: And as the toes of the feet were part of iron, and part of clay, so the kingdom shall be partly strong, and partly broken.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod autem vidisti ferrum mistum testæ ex luto, commiscebuntur quidem humano semine, sed non adhærebunt sibi, sicut ferrum misceri non potest testæ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: (די) [וְדִ֣י] חֲזַ֗יְתָ פַּרְזְלָא֙ מְעָרַב֙ בַּחֲסַ֣ף טִינָ֔א מִתְעָרְבִ֤ין לֶהֱוֺן֙ בִּזְרַ֣ע אֲנָשָׁ֔א וְלָֽא־לֶהֱוֺ֥ן דָּבְקִ֖ין דְּנָ֣ה עִם־דְּנָ֑ה הֵֽא־כְדִ֣י פַרְזְלָ֔א לָ֥א מִתְעָרַ֖ב עִם־חַסְפָּֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: And whereas thou sawest the iron mixed with miry clay, they shall be mingled indeed together with the seed of man, but they shall not stick fast one to another, as iron cannot be mixed with clay.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja ettäs näit raudan sekoitetun savella, niin he kyllä ihmisen siemenellä sekoitetaan, mutta ei he kuitenkaan riipu kiinni toinen toisessansa; niinkuin ei rautaa taideta sekoittaa saven kanssa yhteen.
verses.king_james: And whereas thou sawest iron mixed with miry clay, they shall mingle themselves with the seed of men: but they shall not cleave one to another, even as iron is not mixed with clay.

verses.latin_vulgate: In diebus autem regnorum illorum suscitabit Deus cæli regnum, quod in æternum non dissipabitur, et regnum ejus alteri populo non tradetur : comminuet autem, et consumet universa regna hæc, et ipsum stabit in æternum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּֽבְיוֹמֵיה֞וֹן דִּ֧י מַלְכַיָּ֣א אִנּ֗וּן יְקִים֩ אֱלָ֨הּ שְׁמַיָּ֤א מַלְכוּ֙ דִּ֤י לְעָלְמִין֙ לָ֣א תִתְחַבַּ֔ל וּמַ֨לְכוּתָ֔ה לְעַ֥ם אׇחֳרָ֖ן לָ֣א תִשְׁתְּבִ֑ק תַּדִּ֤ק וְתָסֵיף֙ כׇּל־אִלֵּ֣ין מַלְכְוָתָ֔א וְהִ֖יא תְּק֥וּם לְעָלְמַיָּֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: But in the days of those kingdoms, the God of heaven will set up a kingdom that shall never be destroyed, and his kingdom shall not be delivered up to another people: and it shall break in pieces, and shall consume all these kingdoms: and itself shall stand for ever.
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta näiden kuningasten aikana on Jumala taivaasta yhden valtakunnan asettava, jota ei ikänä kukisteta, ja hänen valtakuntansa ei pidä toiselle kansalle annettaman: sen pitää kaikki nämät valtakunnat särkemän ja hajoittaman, mutta sen pitää ijankaikkisesti pysymän.
verses.king_james: And in the days of these kings shall the God of heaven set up a kingdom, which shall never be destroyed: and the kingdom shall not be left to other people, but it shall break in pieces and consume all these kingdoms, and it shall stand for ever.

verses.latin_vulgate: Secundum quod vidisti, quod de monte abscissus est lapis sine manibus, et comminuit testam, et ferrum, et æs, et argentum, et aurum, Deus magnus ostendit regi quæ ventura sunt postea : et verum est somnium, et fidelis interpretatio ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כׇּל־קֳבֵ֣ל דִּֽי־חֲזַ֡יְתָ דִּ֣י מִטּוּרָא֩ אִתְגְּזֶ֨רֶת אֶ֜בֶן דִּי־לָ֣א בִידַ֗יִן וְ֠הַדֵּ֠קֶת פַּרְזְלָ֨א נְחָשָׁ֤א חַסְפָּא֙ כַּסְפָּ֣א וְדַהֲבָ֔א אֱלָ֥הּ רַב֙ הוֹדַ֣ע לְמַלְכָּ֔א מָ֛ה דִּ֥י לֶהֱוֵ֖א אַחֲרֵ֣י דְנָ֑ה וְיַצִּ֥יב חֶלְמָ֖א וּמְהֵימַ֥ן פִּשְׁרֵֽהּ׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: According as thou sawest, that the stone was cut out of the mountain without hands, and broke in pieces the clay and the iron, and the brass, and the silver, and the gold, the great God hath shewn the king what shall come to pass hereafter, and the dream is true, and the interpretation thereof is faithful.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niinkuin sinä näit kiven, ilman käsiä vuoresta temmatun, joka raudan, vasken, saven, hopian ja kullan murensi: niin on suuri Jumala kuninkaalle näyttänyt, kuinka tästälähin tapahtuman pitää; ja tämä on totinen uni ja sen selitys on oikia.
verses.king_james: Forasmuch as thou sawest that the stone was cut out of the mountain without hands, and that it brake in pieces the iron, the brass, the clay, the silver, and the gold; the great God hath made known to the king what shall come to pass hereafter: and the dream is certain, and the interpretation thereof sure.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc rex Nabuchodonosor cecidit in faciem suam, et Danielem adoravit, et hostias, et incensum præcepit ut sacrificarent ei.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בֵּ֠אדַ֠יִן מַלְכָּ֤א נְבֽוּכַדְנֶצַּר֙ נְפַ֣ל עַל־אַנְפּ֔וֹהִי וּלְדָנִיֵּ֖אל סְגִ֑ד וּמִנְחָה֙ וְנִ֣יחֹחִ֔ין אֲמַ֖ר לְנַסָּ֥כָה לֵֽהּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then king Nabuchodonosor fell on his face, and worshipped Daniel, and commanded that they should offer in sacrifice to him victims and incense.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin lankesi kuningas Nebukadnetsar kasvoillensa, ja kumarsi Danielia, ja käski tehdä hänelle ruokauhria ja polttouhria.
verses.king_james: Then the king Nebuchadnezzar fell upon his face, and worshipped Daniel, and commanded that they should offer an oblation and sweet odours unto him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Loquens ergo rex, ait Danieli : Vere Deus vester Deus deorum est, et Dominus regum, et revelans mysteria : quoniam tu potuisti aperire hoc sacramentum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עָנֵה֩ מַלְכָּ֨א לְדָנִיֵּ֜אל וְאָמַ֗ר מִן־קְשֹׁט֙ דִּ֣י אֱלָהֲכ֗וֹן ה֣וּא אֱלָ֧הּ אֱלָהִ֛ין וּמָרֵ֥א מַלְכִ֖ין וְגָלֵ֣ה רָזִ֑ין דִּ֣י יְכֵ֔לְתָּ לְמִגְלֵ֖א רָזָ֥א דְנָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the king spoke to Daniel, and said: Verily, your God is the God of gods, and Lord of kings, and a revealer of hidden things: seeing thou couldst discover this secret.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja kuningas vastasi Danielia ja sanoi: totisesti on teidän Jumalanne kaikkein jumalain Jumala ja kaikkein kuningasten Herra, joka salaiset asiat ilmoittaa, ettäs tämän salaisen asian olet ilmoittaa taitanut.
verses.king_james: The king answered unto Daniel, and said, Of a truth it is, that your God is a God of gods, and a Lord of kings, and a revealer of secrets, seeing thou couldest reveal this secret.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc rex Danielem in sublime extulit, et munera multa et magna dedit ei : et constituit eum principem super omnes provincias Babylonis, et præfectum magistratuum super cunctos sapientes Babylonis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֱדַ֨יִן מַלְכָּ֜א לְדָנִיֵּ֣אל רַבִּ֗י וּמַתְּנָ֨ן רַבְרְבָ֤ן שַׂגִּיאָן֙ יְהַב־לֵ֔הּ וְהַ֨שְׁלְטֵ֔הּ עַ֖ל כׇּל־מְדִינַ֣ת בָּבֶ֑ל וְרַ֨ב־סִגְנִ֔ין עַ֖ל כׇּל־חַכִּימֵ֥י בָבֶֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: Then the king advanced Daniel to a high station, and gave him many and great gifts: and he made him governor over all the provinces of Babylon: and chief of the magistrates over all the wise men of Babylon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin kuningas korotti Danielin, ja antoi hänelle suuria ja paljon lahjoja, ja asetti hänen kaikkein Babelin valtakuntain päälle ja päämieheksi kaikkein viisasten päälle Babelissa.
verses.king_james: Then the king made Daniel a great man, and gave him many great gifts, and made him ruler over the whole province of Babylon, and chief of the governors over all the wise men of Babylon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Daniel autem postulavit a rege, et constituit super opera provinciæ Babylonis Sidrach, Misach, et Abdenago : ipse autem Daniel erat in foribus regis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְדָנִיֵּאל֙ בְּעָ֣א מִן־מַלְכָּ֔א וּמַנִּ֗י עַ֤ל עֲבִֽידְתָּא֙ דִּ֚י מְדִינַ֣ת בָּבֶ֔ל לְשַׁדְרַ֥ךְ מֵישַׁ֖ךְ וַעֲבֵ֣ד נְג֑וֹ וְדָנִיֵּ֖אל בִּתְרַ֥ע מַלְכָּֽא׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And Daniel requested of the king, and he appointed Sidrach, Misach, and Abdenago, over the works of the province of Babylon: but Daniel himself was in the king's palace.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Daniel rukoili kuningasta, että hän pani Babelin maakuntain päälle Sadrakin, Mesakin ja Abednegon; mutta Daniel itse oli kuninkaan tykönä hänen kartanossansa.
verses.king_james: Then Daniel requested of the king, and he set Shadrach, Meshach, and Abed–nego, over the affairs of the province of Babylon: but Daniel sat in the gate of the king.
